preamble: The nasalized low vowel [ã] diphthongizes to [ie] through raising and fronting, creating a rising diphthong. This shift is rare in documented Romance dialects, as nasal vowels historically resisted vowel breaking processes that affected oral vowels. When attested, it occurs through stages of denasalization followed by standard diphthongization patterns, particularly in peripheral Romance dialects.

examples.context: Rare diphthongization in some Northern Italian or Franco-Provençal dialects; poorly documented
Vowel breaking in open syllables; nasal syllables RESISTED this change, vowels raised instead
In nasal syllables, vowels raised rather than broke; [ã] not documented as breaking to [ie]
Sporadic diphthongization in some Franco-Provençal dialects; limited attestation
